column1: Unchanged — garage org margins already strong at 34.6% – 49%.
GYM INSTALL — UPDATED (Gym-Zone Flooring Only on 1RM)
1RM flooring scoped to GYM ZONE ONLY (~200 sq ft / 8 cases = $960). Was full garage ($1,920). Clients add full-garage upgrade a la carte.
1RM materials: Gym-zone flooring $960 + Wall storage hardware $150 + Deadlift platform lumber $200 = $1,310
COMBO PACKAGES — UPDATED (Accurate Stacked Labor + Gym-Zone Flooring)
Dean's List cost: Grad labor $602 + 1RM labor $1,032 + Grad materials $399 + Gym materials $1,310 + Haul $150 = $3,493
Valedictorian cost: Doc labor $976 + 1RM labor $1,032 + Doc materials $661 + Gym materials $1,310 + Haul $300 = $4,279
Both combos save clients ~$500-600 vs buying separate. Clean value prop without killing your margins.
Full-Garage Flooring Upgrade: For 1RM / combo clients who want to extend gym-zone tiles to cover entire 2-car garage. Price this at $1,497 minimum to stay margin-positive.
Heavy Duty (+20% floor cov / vertical stack)
Key change: Gym-zone flooring on 1RM saved $960 in materials. Moderate price bumps ($300-$500) combined with cost reduction = massive margin jump.
ALL SERVICES RANKED BY MARGIN — FINAL
ALL SERVICES RANKED BY PROFIT $ — FINAL
EVERY CORE SERVICE NOW 34%+ MARGIN. No service below 34.6% (Undergrad). Gym + Combo tiers all 46%+.
VALEDICTORIAN = #1 PROFIT JOB: $3,718 per job at 46.5% margin. Was $2,458 at 32.8%. +$1,260 more profit per booking.
DEAN'S LIST JUMPED 72% IN PROFIT: $3,004 per job (was $1,744). Moderate $500 price bump + cost reduction.
1 REP MAX DOUBLED PROFIT: $2,455 per job (was $1,195). Only +$300 price increase — the gym-zone flooring scope did the heavy lifting.
GYM-ZONE FLOORING = THE KEY MOVE: Scoping flooring to gym area (~200sf) instead of full garage saves $960/job. Full garage available as $1,497 a la carte upgrade.
BUNDLE DISCOUNTS INTACT: Dean's List saves client $497, Valedictorian saves $597. Clean value prop without giving away margin.
PRICE INCREASES WERE MODEST: +$300 on 1RM, +$500 on each combo. Not sticker shock — the cost reduction did most of the work.
